name: RABBIT-FILE-BUG-33
title: branch_ops does not auto-propagate bug-backlog-files through chained workspaces
description:
In a chained-workspace topology (wsN→wsN-1→...→GitHub), _ensure_branch calls 'git ls-remote --heads origin bug-backlog-files' which only sees the immediate origin's local branches (refs/heads/*). If the branch exists as a remote tracking ref in the intermediate (refs/remotes/origin/bug-backlog-files) but not as a local branch, ls-remote returns nothing and the BUG-32 guard raises a hard error requiring manual operator intervention.

Root cause: git's smart protocol, when talking to a local-path remote, serves only that repo's own local refs, not its remote tracking refs. So the branch can exist in wsN-2 and be tracked in wsN-1 as remotes/origin/bug-backlog-files, yet wsN is completely blind to it via both ls-remote and fetch.

BUG-32 fixed the symptom (silent orphan creation) but not the cause. The remediation in the error message (git -C {origin} fetch && git -C {origin} checkout {BRANCH}) is manual operator work that should not be required.

Expected behavior: _ensure_branch should detect when the branch exists as a tracking ref in the intermediate (git -C {origin_url} show-ref refs/remotes/origin/bug-backlog-files) and auto-create the local branch there (git -C {origin_url} branch bug-backlog-files origin/bug-backlog-files) before retrying, so that new workspaces in the chain work without any manual setup.

history.note: Temporarily closed before CONTRACT-BACKLOG-19 housekeeping wave to prevent accidental closure by wave b/b filings; will be reopened after the wave completes

Premise invalidated: chained workspaces are no longer supported per project owner directive (each workspace MUST be standalone and self-contained). The proposed fix (adding auto-propagation logic to branch_ops) would solve a problem the project has declared out-of-scope. Filing follow-up backlog to remove chained-workspace code/spec/test cruft from rabbit-file.
